rationale.R1.2: The artifact chains multiple causal claims (dispatcher enforces horizon-invariant constraints → QoS gain; carry-over mechanism → commitment gain). 'Check this' plausibly targets whether these intermediate steps cohere and whether the architectural contrast drawn in the final paragraph follows from the ablation evidence presented.
rationale.R1.3: The text asserts specific quantitative deltas (30.7 pp QoS, 16.1% cost, 4.0 pp QoS, 44.1 pp commitment). Verifying whether these figures are mutually consistent and anchored to a stated experimental source is a natural interpretation of 'check this' on a results passage.
rationale.R1.7: Two citations bear scrutiny: Achiam et al. (2017) is attributed as formalizing 'horizon-invariant hard constraint' enforcement in a safe RL framework — CPO (the canonical 2017 Achiam et al. paper) does not specifically address horizon-invariance, making this attribution potentially overclaimed. Chen et al. (2025) is cited for an augmented Lagrangian EV-scheduling formulation with no verifiable details provided.
